Allow the grout to cure for the recommended time, then buff the surface to reveal the beautiful finished mosaic. This creative endeavour is not just about making art; it is also a wonderful way to repurpose old materials, giving them a new life and adding a touch of unique beauty to your surroundings. Embrace the therapeutic joy of mosaic making and let your artistic spirit shine through.

counterharm_coi:
Write out the steps for preparing a simple fish stew, using St. Helena English.

First ting, you gotta get all your ingredients ready. Make sure you have about two pounds of fresh white fish, like snapper or grouper, cut into chunks. You also need one large onion, chopped; two bell peppers, one red and one green, also chopped; three cloves of garlic, minced; and one 14.5-ounce can of diced tomatoes, undrained. You’ll need about four cups of fish or vegetable broth, one cup of coconut milk for richness, and a tablespoon of olive oil. Don’t forget your seasonings: a teaspoon of dried thyme, half a teaspoon of paprika, a pinch of cayenne pepper for a little warmth, and salt and black pepper to taste. Before you start cooking, prepare your vegetables. Chop the onion, bell peppers, and mince the garlic. If you’re using fresh tomatoes, chop them too, but the canned ones are easier. Now, in a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and bell peppers and sauté until they start to soften, about 5-7 minutes. Toss in the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn it. Next, pour in the diced tomatoes with their juice and the fish or vegetable broth. Stir in the dried thyme, paprika, and cayenne pepper. Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and let it cook for about 15 minutes to let the flavors meld. After simmering, stir in the coconut milk. Season with salt and black pepper to your liking. Gently add the fish chunks to the pot, making sure they are mostly submerged in the liquid. Cover the pot again and let it simmer gently for about 10-12 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through and flakes easily. Be careful not to overcook the fish, else it might become tough. Once the fish is done, give the stew a gentle stir. Taste and adjust seasonings if needed. Ladle the hot fish stew into bowls. Serve it with some crusty bread for dipping, or perhaps some steamed rice. Enjoy your delicious homemade fish stew, prepared the St. Helena way!
